task: OpenClaw universal high-quality answers through ChatGPT Codex subscription

requirements.requirement: Find the root cause of the market-answer abstention
requirements.evidence: Production route audit showed Claude completed web_search but verified delivery discarded the answer with evidence_authority_insufficient

requirements.requirement: Use the existing ChatGPT subscription without OpenAI API billing
requirements.evidence: Mac Mini Codex CLI reports Logged in using ChatGPT; subprocess removes OPENAI_API_KEY and CODEX_API_KEY, uses gpt-5.5, ephemeral mode, and read-only sandbox

requirements.requirement: Route normal read-only questions through the subscription model regardless of keyword coverage
requirements.evidence: Common codex_subscription_answer route runs before the legacy intent classifier; explicit mutations and governance blocks remain on controlled routes

requirements.requirement: Keep current-fact answers useful and sourced
requirements.evidence: Recency-sensitive subscription answers require an HTTP source URL; a real market run included KOSPI, KOSDAQ, US futures, time-zone distinction, check time, interpretation, and source URLs

requirements.requirement: Do not silently fall back to separately billed APIs
requirements.evidence: Subscription failure returns an explicit no-paid-fallback response; grounded adapters fall back only to local Ollama

requirements.requirement: Verify the deployed Slack user entrypoint
requirements.evidence: Production run delivered 2111 characters through codex_subscription_answer with model=gpt-5.5-chatgpt-subscription; Slack listener restarted PID 64642

governance_bootstrap.summary: Applied source-of-truth deployment, completion evidence, and real-entrypoint verification rules.
Applied Mac Mini ingress ownership, prompt-injection defense, secrets, and selective deployment rules.

verification.target: Mac Mini Slack listener log and OpenClaw route audit

verification.result: confirmed premium_tool_agent called web_search and generated a response; delivery then blocked solely for evidence_authority_insufficient

verification.result: passed: Logged in using ChatGPT; gpt-5.5 executed through provider=openai without API credentials

verification.name: Real current-market answer

verification.result: passed at 2026-07-22 21:25 KST: produced conclusion-first Korean briefing with KOSPI/KOSDAQ, US market timing, observation versus interpretation, and three direct source URLs

verification.name: OpenClaw quality regressions

verification.result: passed: final pre-push pytest 117, contract safety 240/240, E2E golden 10/10; one third-party deprecation warning

verification.result: passed at 2026-07-22 21:31 KST: 2111-character sourced market brief delivered; route=codex_subscription_answer, model=gpt-5.5-chatgpt-subscription, contract decision=deliver; listener PID 64642 running

deploy.result: passed: commit 9398562 pushed to origin/main; backup scratch/pre_deploy_backup_20260722_212916; all four deployed path diffs 0; production run and listener restart passed

residual_risk: ChatGPT subscription usage remains subject to the plan's usage limits and Codex service availability.
OpenClaw native Codex plugin import is blocked for an existing workspace; the implementation safely invokes the already authenticated Codex CLI instead.
The OpenClaw plugin registry still reports a pre-existing Slack metadata conflict warning.
One minimal gpt-5.5 API request was made before the subscription-only requirement was clarified; production normal-answer routing removes API credentials and does not use that path.
